stem: The kinetic energy, in joules, of an object with mass 5 kilograms traveling at a speed of v meters per second is given by the function K, where Kv=52v2. Which of the following is the best interpretation of K20=1000 in this context?

answerOptions.content: The object traveling at 200 meters per second has a kinetic energy of 1,000 joules.
The object traveling at 20 meters per second has a kinetic energy of 1,000 joules.
The object traveling at 1,000 meters per second has a kinetic energy of 20 joules.
The object traveling at 400 meters per second has a kinetic energy of 20 joules.

rationale:
Choice B is correct. In the function Kv, v represents the speed in meters per second and the output is the kinetic energy in joules. Therefore K20=1000 means that at a speed of 20 m/s the kinetic energy is 1,000 joules.

Choices A, C, and D are incorrect.
